unions.name: Response
unions.comment: Represents the responses for the batched operations.

values.name: batch
values.comment: Batches a list of queries and commands to be executed in unison.
values.type: List.List (Cqrs.Operation data error msg) -> List.List (Cqrs.Response error data msg)

name: Cqrs.Command

unions.name: CommandRequest
unions.comment: Represents the possible types of query which can be executed depending on your needs.

unions.name: CommandResponse
unions.comment: Represents the possible states of a command.

aliases.name: RequestSettings
aliases.type: { body : Json.Encode.Value, defaultError : error, toError : Json.Decode.Decoder error, toMsg : Cqrs.Command.CommandResponse error -> msg, url : String.String }

aliases.name: RequestTaskSettings
aliases.type: { body : Json.Encode.Value, defaultError : error, toError : Json.Decode.Decoder error, url : String.String }

aliases.name: RequestTaskWithConfigurationSettings
aliases.type: { body : Json.Encode.Value, config : Cqrs.Command.TaskRequestConfiguration, defaultError : error, toError : Json.Decode.Decoder error, url : String.String }

aliases.name: RequestWithConfigurationSettings
aliases.type: { body : Json.Encode.Value, config : Cqrs.Command.StandardRequestConfiguration, defaultError : error, toError : Json.Decode.Decoder error, toMsg : Cqrs.Command.CommandResponse error -> msg, url : String.String }

aliases.name: StandardRequestConfiguration
aliases.comment: Configuration for requests resulting in a `Cmd`
aliases.type: { headers : List.List Http.Header, timeout : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Float, tracker : Maybe.Maybe String.String, risky : Basics.Bool }

aliases.name: TaskRequestConfiguration
aliases.comment: Configuration for requests resulting in a `Task`
aliases.type: { headers : List.List Http.Header, timeout : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Float, risky : Basics.Bool }

values.name: decoder
values.comment: Decodes a given payload into a `Cqrs.Command.CommandResponse error`.

values.name: fail
values.comment:
Constructs the `Failed` variant of a `Cqrs.Command.CommandResponse error`.

This is useful for testing how your UI will respond to the sad path, without actually sending a command.

values.name: failed
values.comment:
Checks if a given `Cqrs.Command.CommandResponse error` is a `Failed` variant.

    failed Succeeded --> False

    failed (Failed "reason") --> True

values.name: fromRemoteData
values.comment: Converts a given `WebData (CommandResponse error)` into a `Cqrs.Command.CommandResponse error`.

values.name: fromResult
values.comment: Converts a `Result error data` to a `Cqrs.Command.CommandResponse error`

values.name: mapError
values.comment: Maps the `Failed` variant of a given `Cqrs.Command.CommandResponse error`.

values.name: partition
values.comment: Partitions a series of `CommandResponse error` instances into a tuple of successful and unsuccessful responses.

values.name: reason
values.comment:
If a given `Cqrs.Command.CommandResponse error` was unsuccessful, the reason for the failure will be returned.

    reason Succeeded --> Nothing

    reason (Failed "reason") --> Just "reason"

values.name: request
values.comment: Sends a command to the given URL with the provided body and returns a parsed `Cqrs.Command.CommandResponse error` in turn.

values.name: requestTask
values.comment:
Sends a command to the given URL and returns a `Task` containing the parsed `Cqrs.Command.CommandResponse error`.
The value contained within the `Cqrs.Command.CommandResponse error`, will be the value parsed via the provided decoder.

values.name: requestTaskWithConfiguration
values.comment: Similar to `requestTask` but a `TaskRequestConfig` can be passed in for cases where customisation of the request headers, etc are desired.

values.name: requestWithConfiguration
values.comment: Similar to `request` but a `StandardRequestConfig` can be passed in for cases where customisation of the request headers, etc are desired.

values.name: succeed
values.comment:
Constructs the `Succeeded` variant of a `Cqrs.Command.CommandResponse error`.

This is useful for testing how your UI will respond to the happy path, without actually sending a command.

values.name: succeeded
values.comment:
Checks if a given `Cqrs.Command.CommandResponse error` is a `Succeeded` variant.

    succeeded Succeeded --> True

    succeeded (Failed "reason") --> False

values.name: toMaybe
values.comment: Converts a `Cqrs.Command.CommandResponse error` to a `Maybe ()`

values.name: toResult
values.comment: Converts a `Cqrs.Command.CommandResponse error` to a `Result error data`

values.name: unpack
values.comment: Convert a `CommandResponse error` to a `value` by applying either the first function if the `CommandResponse error` is an `Failed` variant or the second function if the `CommandResponse error` is a `Succeeded` variant.

values.name: unwrap
values.comment: Convert a `CommandResponse error` to a `value` by applying a function if the `CommandResponse error` is a `Succeeded` variant or using the provided default value if it is an `Failed` variant.

name: Cqrs.Query

unions.name: QueryRequest
unions.comment: Represents the possible types of query which can be executed depending on your needs.

unions.name: QueryResponse
unions.comment: Represents the possible states of a query.

aliases.name: RequestSettings
aliases.type: { defaultError : error, toData : Json.Decode.Decoder data, toError : Json.Decode.Decoder error, toMsg : Cqrs.Query.QueryResponse error data -> msg, url : String.String }

aliases.name: RequestTaskSettings
aliases.type: { defaultError : error, toData : Json.Decode.Decoder data, toError : Json.Decode.Decoder error, url : String.String }

aliases.name: RequestTaskWithConfigurationSettings
aliases.type: { config : Cqrs.Query.TaskRequestConfiguration, defaultError : error, toData : Json.Decode.Decoder data, toError : Json.Decode.Decoder error, url : String.String }

aliases.name: RequestWithConfigurationSettings
aliases.type: { config : Cqrs.Query.StandardRequestConfiguration, defaultError : error, toData : Json.Decode.Decoder data, toError : Json.Decode.Decoder error, toMsg : Cqrs.Query.QueryResponse error data -> msg, url : String.String }

aliases.name: StandardRequestConfiguration
aliases.comment: Configuration for requests resulting in a `Cmd`
aliases.type: { headers : List.List Http.Header, timeout : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Float, tracker : Maybe.Maybe String.String, risky : Basics.Bool }

aliases.name: TaskRequestConfiguration
aliases.comment: Configuration for requests resulting in a `Task`
aliases.type: { headers : List.List Http.Header, timeout : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Float, risky : Basics.Bool }

values.name: data
values.comment:
If a given `QueryResponse error data` was successful, the data it contains will be returned.

    data (Data []) --> Just []

    data (Error "reason") --> Nothing

values.name: decoder
values.comment: Decodes a given payload into a `QueryResponse error data`.
values.type: Json.Decode.Decoder data -> Json.Decode.Decoder error -> Json.Decode.Decoder (Cqrs.Query.QueryResponse error data)

values.name: fail
values.comment:
Constructs the `Error` variant of a `QueryResponse error data`.

This is useful for testing how your UI will respond to the sad path, without actually sending a query, for example.

values.name: failed
values.comment:
Checks if a given `QueryResponse error data` is an `Error` variant.

    failed (Data []) --> False

    failed (Error "reason") --> True

values.name: filter
values.comment: Filter a given `QueryResponse error data` if it is in the `Data` variant and should the predicate not pass, map to a `Error` variant with the given error. Useful for error value mapping within a given domain.
values.type: error -> (data -> Basics.Bool) -> Cqrs.Query.QueryResponse error data -> Cqrs.Query.QueryResponse error data

values.name: fromRemoteData
values.comment: Converts a given `WebData (QueryResponse issue data)` into a `QueryResponse error data`.
values.type: error -> RemoteData.WebData (Cqrs.Query.QueryResponse error data) -> Cqrs.Query.QueryResponse error data

values.name: fromResult
values.comment: Converts a `Result error data` to a `QueryResponse error data`
values.type: Result.Result error data -> Cqrs.Query.QueryResponse error data

values.name: map
values.comment: Maps the `Data` variant of a given `QueryResponse error data`.
values.type: (data -> nextData) -> Cqrs.Query.QueryResponse error data -> Cqrs.Query.QueryResponse error nextData

values.name: mapBoth
values.comment: Maps both the `Data` and the `Error` variant of a given `QueryResponse error data`.
values.type: (error -> nextError) -> (data -> nextData) -> Cqrs.Query.QueryResponse error data -> Cqrs.Query.QueryResponse nextError nextData

values.name: mapError
values.comment: Maps the `Error` variant of a given `QueryResponse error data`.
values.type: (error -> nextError) -> Cqrs.Query.QueryResponse error data -> Cqrs.Query.QueryResponse nextError data

values.name: merge
values.comment: When a given `QueryResponse error data` is actually in the form `QueryResponse value value` then we can return the underlying value from either state. This is especially useful when the error and value have been mapped to domain types.

values.name: partition
values.comment: Partitions a series of `QueryResponse error data` instances into a tuple of successful and unsuccessful responses.
values.type: List.List (Cqrs.Query.QueryResponse error data) -> ( List.List data, List.List error )

values.name: reason
values.comment:
If a given `QueryResponse error data` was unsuccessful, the reason for the failure will be returned.

    reason (Data []) --> Nothing

    reason (Error "reason") --> Just "reason"

values.name: request
values.comment: Sends a query to the given URL and returns a `Cmd` for the given `msg` containing the parsed `QueryResponse error data`.
values.type: Cqrs.Query.RequestSettings data error msg -> Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg

values.name: requestTask
values.comment: Sends a query to the given URL and returns a `Task` which will contain the parsed `QueryResponse error data`.
values.type: Cqrs.Query.RequestTaskSettings data error -> Task.Task () (Cqrs.Query.QueryResponse error data)

values.name: requestTaskWithConfiguration
values.comment: Similar to `requestTask` but a `TaskRequestConfig` can be passed in for cases where customisation of the request headers, etc are desired.

values.name: requestWithConfiguration
values.comment: Similar to `request` but a `StandardRequestConfig` can be passed in for cases where customisation of the request headers, etc are desired.

values.name: succeed
values.comment:
Constructs the `Data` variant of a `QueryResponse error data`.

This is useful for testing how your UI will respond to the happy path, without actually sending a query, for example.

values.name: succeeded
values.comment:
Checks if a given `QueryResponse error data` is a `Data` variant.

    succeeded (Data []) --> True

    succeeded (Error "reason") --> False

values.name: toMaybe
values.comment: Converts a `QueryResponse error data` to a `Maybe data`

values.name: toResult
values.comment: Converts a `QueryResponse error data` to a `Result error data`
values.type: Cqrs.Query.QueryResponse error data -> Result.Result error data

values.name: unpack
values.comment: Convert a `QueryResponse error data` to a `value` by applying either the first function if the `QueryResponse error data` is an `Error` variant or the second function if the `QueryResponse error data` is a `Data` variant.
values.type: (error -> value) -> (data -> value) -> Cqrs.Query.QueryResponse error data -> value

values.name: unwrap
values.comment: Convert a `QueryResponse error data` to a `value` by applying a function if the `QueryResponse error data` is a `Data` variant or using the provided default value if it is an `Error` variant.
values.type: value -> (data -> value) -> Cqrs.Query.QueryResponse error data -> value

values.name: withDefault
values.comment: Provides a default case for a `QueryResponse error data` in place of the value held within the `Error` variant.
